content.s1_claims_discipline_internal:
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TAGS: hair, nails, hair loss, alopecia, balding, thinning hair, brittle nails, biotin, collagen, keratin, minoxidil, finasteride, PRP hair, exosomes hair, iron hair, thyroid hair, telogen effluvium, androgenetic, female hair loss, male pattern, receding, shedding, breakage, growth, regrowth, male pattern baldness, hair growth, nail health, collagen hair, DHT hair loss, iron hair loss, thyroid hair loss, postpartum hair loss, anagen effluvium, chronic telogen effluvium, hair restoration, scalp health, seborrheic dermatitis hair, PCOS hair, cortisol hair loss, zinc hair, selenium hair, saw palmetto DHT, scalp PRP, hair follicle miniaturization, hair growth cycle, alopecia areata
# CATEGORY: Systems
# USE FOR: Hair loss, thinning hair, balding, PRP for hair, nail problems, hair growth support
# COUPLED_SYSTEMS: 17_MALE_HORMONES, 18_FEMALE_HORMONES, 21_SKIN, 06_METABOLIC, 33_PEPTIDES, 57_THYROID_COMPLETE, 04_NUTRITION_DIET
# TRIAGE_QUESTIONS: Where is the hair loss happening? When did it start? Any recent stress, surgery, or hormone changes?
# IV_ROLE: IV is NOT first-line for hair loss or nail problems. Priority is identifying root cause (iron/ferritin, thyroid, hormones, stress, nutrition). Oral supplements (biotin, iron, zinc, saw palmetto for androgenetic) and topical treatments are first-line. PRP for hair is more relevant than IV. IV nutrient support may help if severe deficiency is documented but should not be the default recommendation.
# INTERVENTION_PRIORITY: Root cause (labs: ferritin, thyroid, hormones) → Oral supplements (biotin, iron, zinc) → Topical treatments → PRP for hair → IV only if severe deficiency documented
# TELECONSULT_LIMITS: CAN do remotely WITH PHOTOS: pattern assessment, supplement protocol, lab ordering (ferritin, thyroid, hormones), minoxidil guidance, follow-up with progress photos. CANNOT do remotely: scalp examination (trichoscopy), PRP procedure, scalp biopsy. Photos at consistent angle/lighting improve remote tracking.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# HAIR, SKIN & NAIL HEALTH
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

 === SECTION 1: CLAIMS DISCIPLINE [INTERNAL_ONLY] ===
## WINDOWS TO INTERNAL HEALTH

Your hair, skin, and nails reflect what's happening inside your body.

**Changes often signal:**
- Nutritional deficiencies
- Hormonal imbalances
- Thyroid dysfunction
- Gut health issues
- Inflammation
- Stress
- Toxin exposure

**Treating from the inside out creates lasting results.**

---

## HAIR HEALTH

### Hair Loss Basics

**Normal:** Losing 50-100 hairs per day is normal.

**Hair growth cycle:**
- **Anagen (Growth):** 2-7 years — 85-90% of hair
- **Catagen (Transition):** 2-3 weeks
- **Telogen (Resting/Shedding):** 3-4 months — Then falls out

**Hair loss occurs when:** More hairs enter telogen, anagen shortens, 
or follicles miniaturize.

### Types of Hair Loss

| Type | Pattern | Cause |
|------|---------|-------|
| **Androgenetic alopecia** | Pattern baldness (temples, crown) | Genetic + DHT |
| **Telogen effluvium** | Diffuse shedding | Stress, illness, nutritional |
| **Alopecia areata** | Patchy | Autoimmune |
| **Thyroid-related** | Diffuse | Hypothyroid or hyperthyroid |
| **Nutritional** | Diffuse | Iron, zinc, biotin, protein deficiency |
| **Hormonal (women)** | Diffuse or pattern | PCOS, menopause, postpartum |

### Root Causes to Investigate

| Factor | Testing |
|--------|---------|
| Thyroid | Full panel (TSH, FT3, FT4, antibodies) |
| Iron | Ferritin (target >70 for hair) |
| Zinc | Plasma zinc |
| Vitamin D | 25-OH vitamin D |
| B12/Folate | Serum levels |
| Hormones | Testosterone, DHT, DHEA-S |
| Protein | Diet assessment |
| Stress | History, cortisol if indicated |
| Gut | GI-MAP if malabsorption suspected |

### Hair Support Protocol

**Foundation:**
| Supplement | Dose | Purpose |
|------------|------|---------|
| Iron | If ferritin <70 | Essential for hair growth |
| Zinc | 15-30mg | Hair follicle function |
| Biotin | 2500-5000mcg | Keratin production |
| Vitamin D | Optimize 50-80 | Hair cycling |
| B-Complex | Per product | Overall hair health |
| Protein | 1+ g/kg | Building block |

**Enhanced:**
| Supplement | Dose | Purpose |
|------------|------|---------|
| Collagen | 10-15g | Hair structure |
| Saw palmetto | 320mg | DHT blocker (androgenetic) |
| Pumpkin seed oil | 400mg | DHT inhibitor |
| Silica | 5-10mg | Structural support |
| MSM | 1-3g | Sulfur for keratin |
| Omega-3 | 2g | Scalp health |

**Topical options:**
- Minoxidil (FDA-approved)
- Rosemary oil (comparable to minoxidil in studies)
- Ketoconazole shampoo
- Low-level laser therapy (LLLT)
- PRP (platelet-rich plasma injections)

### Androgenetic Alopecia (Pattern Hair Loss)

**For men and women (different patterns):**

| Intervention | Details |
|--------------|---------|
| DHT blockers (internal) | Saw palmetto, pumpkin seed oil |
| Minoxidil | Topical 2-5% |
| Finasteride (men) | Prescription, blocks DHT |
| Spironolactone (women) | Anti-androgen |
| Rosemary oil | Topical massage |
| PRP | Injections q4-6 weeks |
| Low-level laser | Home devices or in-office |

### Telogen Effluvium

**Diffuse shedding 2-4 months after trigger.**

**Common triggers:**
- Major stress or illness
- Surgery
- Pregnancy (postpartum)
- Crash dieting
- Thyroid changes
- Starting/stopping medications
- Nutritional deficiency

**Treatment:**
- Identify and address trigger
- Nutritional support (iron, zinc, biotin, protein)
- Stress management
- Time (usually resolves in 6-12 months)
- Support follicle health while recovering

---

## NAIL HEALTH

### What Nails Tell Us

| Sign | Possible Meaning |
|------|------------------|
| Brittle/splitting | Thyroid, biotin deficiency, dehydration |
| Ridges (vertical) | Often normal aging |
| Ridges (horizontal/Beau's lines) | Illness, stress, nutritional deficiency |
| Spoon-shaped (koilonychia) | Iron deficiency |
| White spots | Usually minor trauma, sometimes zinc |
| Yellow nails | Fungal, respiratory disease, nicotine |
| Pale nails | Anemia |
| Clubbing | Lung or heart disease |
| Half-and-half nails | Kidney disease |
| Dark lines | Need evaluation (melanoma concern in some) |

### Nail Support

| Supplement | Dose | Purpose |
|------------|------|---------|
| Biotin | 2500-5000mcg | Nail strength |
| Collagen | 10-15g | Structure |
| Silica | 5-10mg | Strength |
| Iron | If deficient | Essential for nails |
| Zinc | 15-30mg | Growth |
| Protein | Adequate | Building block |

### Brittle Nails Protocol

1. **Rule out thyroid** (common cause)
2. **Check iron/ferritin**
3. **Biotin 2500-5000mcg** daily for 3-6 months
4. **Collagen 10-15g** daily
5. **Keep nails moisturized**
6. **Wear gloves for wet work**
7. **Avoid harsh chemicals**

---

## SKIN HEALTH

**See 21_SKIN for comprehensive skin conditions.**

### General Skin Support

**From the inside:**
| Supplement | Dose | Benefit |
|------------|------|---------|
| Collagen | 10-15g | Elasticity, hydration |
| Vitamin C | 500-1000mg | Collagen synthesis |
| Vitamin E | 200-400 IU | Antioxidant |
| Zinc | 15-30mg | Healing, acne |
| Omega-3 | 2-3g | Inflammation, hydration |
| Vitamin A | 5000-10000 IU | Skin cell turnover |
| Astaxanthin | 4-12mg | UV protection, anti-aging |
| Hyaluronic acid | 100-200mg | Hydration |

**From the outside:**
- Sunscreen (daily)
- Retinoid (evening)
- Vitamin C serum (morning)
- Moisturizer
- Gentle cleansing

### Aging Skin

**Internal factors:**
- Collagen decline (1% per year after 20)
- Elastin breakdown
- Oxidative stress
- Glycation
- Hormonal changes
- Inflammation

**Anti-aging protocol:**
| Supplement | Dose |
|------------|------|
| Collagen peptides | 10-15g |
| Vitamin C | 1g |
| Astaxanthin | 4-12mg |
| CoQ10 | 100-200mg |
| Resveratrol | 100-250mg |
| Hyaluronic acid | 100-200mg |
| NAD+ precursors | 250-500mg |

**Topical:**
- Retinoid (gold standard)
- Vitamin C
- Peptides
- Hyaluronic acid
- Niacinamide
- Sunscreen (most important)

### Acne (Summary)

**Root causes:** Hormones, insulin, gut, diet, inflammation, nutrients

**Key interventions:**
- Low glycemic diet
- Eliminate dairy (trial)
- Zinc 30-50mg
- DIM 100-200mg (hormonal)
- Omega-3 2g
- Probiotics
- Vitamin A 10000 IU
- Address gut

**See 21_SKIN for detailed acne protocol.**

---

## COLLAGEN: THE FOUNDATION

### Why Collagen Matters

**Collagen is the most abundant protein in the body:**
- Skin elasticity and hydration
- Hair strength
- Nail structure
- Joints
- Gut lining

**We lose 1-1.5% of collagen per year after age 20.**

### Collagen Supplementation

**Research supports:**
- Improved skin elasticity and hydration
- Reduced wrinkles
- Improved nail growth
- May help hair

**Types:**
| Type | Found In | Benefits |
|------|----------|----------|
| Type I | Skin, bone, tendons | Skin, anti-aging |
| Type II | Cartilage | Joints |
| Type III | Skin, blood vessels | With Type I for skin |

**Dose:** 10-15g daily hydrolyzed collagen peptides

**For best results:**
- Take with vitamin C (needed for collagen synthesis)
- Consistency (minimum 8 weeks to see results)
- Any time of day is fine

---

## SPECIAL SITUATIONS

### Postpartum Hair Loss

**Normal:** Hair loss 2-4 months postpartum (catching up from pregnancy)

**Support:**
- Continue prenatal vitamins
- Ensure iron adequate
- Protein intake
- Patience (usually resolves by 6-12 months)
- Stress management

### Menopause and Hair/Skin

**Estrogen decline affects:**
- Hair (thinning, loss)
- Skin (dryness, wrinkles, sagging)
- Collagen production

**Support:**
- Consider HRT (helps hair and skin)
- Collagen supplementation
- Topical estrogen (face cream formulas)
- Phytoestrogens (soy, red clover)
- Enhanced supplementation

### GLP-1 Medications and Hair Loss

**Some patients report hair loss on semaglutide/tirzepatide.**

**Causes:**
- Rapid weight loss (telogen effluvium trigger)
- Nutritional deficiency (eating less)
- Protein insufficiency

**Prevention/Treatment:**
- Protein 1.6-2.0 g/kg (critical)
- Full supplementation (biotin, zinc, iron)
- Collagen 15g daily
- Gradual weight loss if possible
- Monitor nutrients

---

 === SECTION 2: PATIENT COMMUNICATION [PATIENT_OK] ===
## HAIR LOSS PATHOPHYSIOLOGY

### Androgenetic Alopecia

```
ANDROGENETIC ALOPECIA MECHANISM

Testosterone
    │
    ▼ (5α-reductase)
    │
   DHT (dihydrotestosterone)
    │
    ▼
Binds androgen receptors in susceptible follicles
    │
    ▼
Follicle miniaturization
    │
    ▼
Shorter anagen phase
    │
    ▼
Thinner, shorter hairs → Eventually vellus hairs
    │
    ▼
Pattern hair loss

SUSCEPTIBILITY:
• Genetic (androgen receptor gene variants)
• Pattern: Men — Temples, crown
• Pattern: Women — Diffuse, especially crown
```

### Telogen Effluvium

```
TELOGEN EFFLUVIUM MECHANISM

TRIGGER (2-4 months before shedding)
├── Physical stress (illness, surgery)
├── Emotional stress
├── Nutritional (crash diet, deficiency)
├── Hormonal (thyroid, postpartum)
├── Medication
└── Fever

    │
    ▼

Hairs prematurely enter telogen phase
(Normally 10-15% in telogen → 30-50% in telogen)

    │
    ▼

2-4 months later: Synchronized shedding
(50-100 hairs/day → 200-300+ hairs/day)

    │
    ▼

RECOVERY: 6-12 months after trigger resolved
New hairs begin growing
```

## CLINICAL PROTOCOLS

### Comprehensive Hair Loss Workup

**History:**
- Onset and duration
- Pattern (diffuse vs. patterned)
- Associated symptoms (fatigue, weight, mood)
- Recent stressors or illness
- Dietary changes
- Medications
- Family history
- Menstrual history (women)

**Physical:**
- Pull test
- Scalp examination
- Pattern assessment
- Signs of other conditions

**Labs:**
| Test | Target/Notes |
|------|--------------|
| Ferritin | >70 ng/mL for hair |
| Iron panel | Complete picture |
| TSH, Free T4, Free T3 | Thyroid assessment |
| Thyroid antibodies | Hashimoto's |
| Zinc | Plasma |
| Vitamin D | >50 ng/mL |
| B12, Folate | Deficiency can cause hair loss |
| CBC | Anemia |
| Testosterone, Free T | If pattern loss or PCOS signs |
| DHEA-S | Androgen assessment |
| DHT | If androgenetic suspected (male) |

### Androgenetic Alopecia Protocol

**Men:**
| Intervention | Details |
|--------------|---------|
| Saw palmetto | 320mg daily |
| Pumpkin seed oil | 400mg daily |
| Minoxidil | 5% topical 2x/day |
| Finasteride | 1mg daily (discuss side effects) |
| Ketoconazole shampoo | 2%, 2-3x/week |
| Rosemary oil | Add to shampoo or scalp massage |
| Low-level laser | Home device, per device protocol |
| PRP | Q4-6 weeks for 3-4 sessions, then q6-12mo |

**Women:**
| Intervention | Details |
|--------------|---------|
| Spironolactone | 100-200mg daily (anti-androgen) |
| Saw palmetto | 320mg daily |
| Minoxidil | 2-5% topical |
| Rosemary oil | Topical |
| Iron optimization | Ferritin >70 |
| Address PCOS if present | See 18_FEMALE |
| PRP | Same as men |

### Telogen Effluvium Protocol

**Phase 1: Identify and Address Trigger**
- Remove trigger if ongoing
- Treat underlying condition
- Correct nutritional deficiency

**Phase 2: Optimize Nutrients**
| Nutrient | Target/Dose |
|----------|-------------|
| Ferritin | >70 ng/mL |
| Protein | 1.2+ g/kg daily |
| Zinc | 30mg daily |
| Biotin | 5000mcg daily |
| Vitamin D | 50-80 ng/mL |
| Collagen | 15g daily |

**Phase 3: Support Recovery**
| Intervention | Details |
|--------------|---------|
| Gentle hair care | Avoid heat, tight styles |
| Scalp massage | Increase circulation |
| Rosemary oil | Topical support |
| Stress management | If stress-triggered |
| Patience | 6-12 months for recovery |

### Nail Protocol

**Assessment:**
- Thyroid function
- Iron/Ferritin
- B12
- Zinc
- Protein intake

**Treatment:**
| Supplement | Dose | Duration |
|------------|------|----------|
| Biotin | 2500-5000mcg | 6+ months |
| Collagen | 10-15g | Ongoing |
| Silica | 10mg | Ongoing |
| Iron | If deficient | Until replete |
| Zinc | 15-30mg | Ongoing |

**Topical:**
- Keep nails moisturized
- Avoid harsh chemicals
- Don't remove cuticles

---

 === SECTION 3: PHARMACOKINETICS & DYNAMICS (ADME) [INTERNAL_ONLY] ===
## HAIR LOSS CAUSES

| Type | Pattern | Key Test |
|------|---------|----------|
| Androgenetic | Pattern (M), Diffuse (F) | Hormones |
| Telogen effluvium | Diffuse | History (trigger 2-4mo ago) |
| Thyroid | Diffuse | Thyroid panel |
| Iron deficiency | Diffuse | Ferritin >70 |
| Nutritional | Diffuse | Various nutrients |

## HAIR SUPPORT ESSENTIALS

| Supplement | Dose |
|------------|------|
| Ferritin | Target >70 |
| Zinc | 15-30mg |
| Biotin | 2500-5000mcg |
| Vitamin D | 50-80 ng/mL |
| Collagen | 10-15g |
| Protein | 1+ g/kg |

## ANDROGENETIC ADD-ONS

- Saw palmetto 320mg
- Minoxidil topical
- Rosemary oil
- PRP injections
- Low-level laser

## NAIL ESSENTIALS

- Biotin 2500-5000mcg
- Collagen 10-15g
- Check thyroid
- Check iron

## SKIN ESSENTIALS

| Internal | Topical |
|----------|---------|
| Collagen 10-15g | Sunscreen |
| Vitamin C 500-1000mg | Retinoid |
| Omega-3 2g | Vitamin C serum |
| Astaxanthin 4-12mg | Moisturizer |

## TIMELINE

- Hair supplements: 3-6 months for results
- Collagen for skin: 8-12 weeks
- Nail improvement: 6+ months (nail growth rate)

---
